mnemonics.title: Power Law Gamma Effects
mnemonics.content: LOW GAMMA = LIGHT (γ < 1 expands dark regions); HIGH GAMMA = DARK (γ > 1 compresses dark regions)

mnemonics.title: Point vs Neighborhood
mnemonics.content: POINT: PIXEL ONLY = INDEPENDENT; NEIGHBOR: NURTURED BY NEIGHBORS = DEPENDENT

acronyms.expansion: Transformation function of input intensity r
acronyms.usage: Fundamental notation for any point processing operation s = T(r)
Used in histogram equalization to map gray levels s = (L-1) × CDF(r)
Highest bit plane (7 in 8-bit) containing most visual information in bit-plane slicing

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a photographic negative where black becomes white and white becomes black. Think of INVERTING colors like a mirror reflection.
Think of a telescope that compresses distant stars into view. Log compresses large bright values while expanding small dark values.
Think of brightness control knob: turning left (γ < 1) makes image brighter (expands darks); turning right (γ > 1) makes it darker (compresses darks).
Imagine stretching a rubber band (intensity range) from narrow to wide. Pixels at ends get pulled apart to fill full 0-255 range.
Stack 8 transparent sheets. Top sheet (MSB) shows main structure; bottom sheets (LSB) add fine detail. Combined they form complete image.

analogies.analogy: Like individually adjusting each lightbulb in a grid to a new brightness, independent of its neighbors. Each pixel gets its own transformation.
Like a volume knob that makes quiet sounds louder and loud sounds less overwhelming. Log transformation does similar for pixel intensities.
Like redistributing people in a city so every neighborhood has equal population. Equalizes the distribution of gray levels.
Like calibrating a TV. The TV doesn't show brightness linearly, so we pre-compensate the signal to get correct display.
Like reading a number: 2 in 127 means 2×1 (least significant), but 1 in 127 means 1×64 (most significant). Each bit contributes differently to final brightness.
Like highlighting specific words in a document by coloring only those words while keeping rest as-is.
